动作与关系：男孩用右手握住女孩左手，两人面朝右侧沿石子路向前走，男孩微低头看向脚边发光的石子，女孩跟随其节奏；路面散布的白色鹅卵石在月光下隐隐发亮，形成可读路径从前景延伸至远处；两人步伐同向，前后相差半步，形成归途行进姿态。

场景：森林深处空地至通往家的土路，夜晚月光照耀过渡至黎明破晓；画面左侧天色深蓝，月亮隐于树冠上方，右侧远处天边透出橙黄晨曦；土路从左下角蜿蜒延伸，路面白色石子清晰可见，两侧高大树木夹道，远处右侧可见木屋低矮轮廓；男孩站在土路中段，右脚略前，左脚重心在后，女孩紧随其右侧半步。

光影与色彩：整体冷蓝月夜色调为主，路面石子处有柔和白色月光反光点；远景天际渐变为暖橙晨色，形成冷暖对比，暗示漫长归途接近尾声；树木阴影深沉，路面有斑驳光斑。

主体与角色：男孩，棕色短直发，深棕色粗布短外套，浅灰色长裤，黑色系带短靴，口袋宽大；站立时头顶约与成人腰部齐平，在画面中占画面高度约1/3，脚边路面石子与其靴子形成近距参照；女孩，棕色中长微弯发，浅蓝色碎花短连衣裙，白色围裙，黑色系带平底鞋；身形略低于男孩，头顶约与男孩肩部齐平；两人身高均明显低于两侧树木，树干高度约为两人身高的4至5倍，形成比例压迫感。

风格与媒介：STYLE_PLACEHOLDER。

约束条件：角色外观、服装细节、场景家具布局严格遵循参考图，不自行添加或修改任何细节，人物外观与比例保持前后一致，画面内不得渲染中文文字，无水印，无多余人物，人物比例自然，道具大小合理，空间关系清楚。单格分镜不加任何外边框，整张画面不加外边框。场景指纹：蜿蜒土路延伸至林缘+路面白色鹅卵石+高大落叶树木夹道，位置形态不变。男孩：发色禁止更改，深棕色粗布外套禁止替换，宽大口袋不得去掉，无胡须无络腮胡。女孩：发色禁止更改，浅蓝色碎花连衣裙禁止替换，白色围裙不得去掉。比例锚点：男孩头顶约与成人腰部齐平，路边树干高度约为两孩子身高的4至5倍，女孩头顶约与男孩肩部齐平。画面内无任何浮动文字、旁白说明文字、标题文字、标签文字。

covered_sentences: They knocked at the door, and when the woman opened it she exclaimed: "You naughty children, what a long time you've slept in the wood! We thought you were never going to come back."
But the father rejoiced, for his felt guilty for leaving his children behind them.

covered_sentences: Not long afterward there was again a great shortage of food in the land, and the children heard their mother speak to their father in bed one night like this: "Everything is eaten up once more; we have only half a loaf of bread in the house, and when that's done it's all up with us.
The children must be got rid of; we'll lead them deeper into the wood this time, so that they won't be able to find their way out again.
There is no other way of saving ourselves."
The man's heart felt heavy, and he thought: "Surely it would be better to share the last bite with one's children!"
But his wife wouldn't listen to his arguments, and they quarrel for a long time until the old man finally agreed that the children had to be got rid of.
Although he was very sad about it.

covered_sentences: But the children were awake, and had heard the conversation.
When the old people were asleep Hansel got up, and wanted to go out and pick up pebbles again, as he had done the first time; but the woman had barred the door, and Hansel couldn't get out.
But he consoled his little sister, and said: "Don't cry, Grettel, and sleep peacefully, for God is sure to help us."

动作与关系：左格：男孩从床铺边站起，上身略前倾，右手伸向右侧厚木板门，手指触及门栓或门板表面，门栓横插状态清晰可见，男孩面部侧向门的方向，表情凝重，眉头轻蹙；无气泡；左格呈现"尝试→受阻"的受困姿态；右格：男孩从门边折返，坐于床沿，上身微低，面对侧卧于床上略哭泣的女孩，右手轻轻搭在女孩肩膀处，面部表情温和带安慰；女孩侧卧，眼眶略红，表情悲伤中带着聆听，头靠枕头；男孩头顶对白气泡内英文 "Don't cry" + 睡眠月亮简笔画，指向女孩。

Then they fell asleep, and evening passed away, but nobody came to the poor children.

At midday they saw a beautiful little snow-white bird sitting on a branch, which sang so sweetly that they stopped still and listened to it.
And when its song was finished it flapped its wings and flew on in front of them.

covered_sentences: "Now we'll hop in," said Hansel, "and stuck our faces with the food. I'll eat a bit of the roof, and you, Grettel, can eat some of the window, which you'll find a tasty little snack."
Hansel stretched up his hand and broke off a little bit of the roof to see what it was like, and Grettel went to the window and began to nibble at it.
At that moment a shrill voice came out from the room inside:
"Nibble, nibble, little mouse, Who's nibbling at my house?"
The children answered: "Tis Heaven's own child, The tempest wild," and went on eating, for they were ever so hungry.

covered_sentences: So the best food was cooked for poor Hansel, but Grettel got nothing but crab-shells.
Every morning the old woman hobbled out to the stable and cried: "Hansel, put out your finger that I may feel if you are getting fat."

"Kind heaven help us now!" she cried; "if only the wild beasts in the wood had eaten us, then at least we should have died together."

covered_sentences: Early in the morning Grettel had to go out and hang up the kettle full of water, and light the fire.
"First we'll make a pie," said the old witch; "I've heated the oven already and rolled the pastry."
She pushed Grettel out to the oven, from which fiery flames were already jumping.
"Creep in," said the witch, "and see if it's properly heated, so that we can shove in the pie."
For when she had got Grettel in she meant to close the oven and let the girl roast, that she might eat her up too.
But Grettel saw through her wicked trick, and said: "I don't know how I'm going to do it; how do I get in?"

covered_sentences: "You silly goose!" said the hag, "the opening is big enough; see, I could get in myself," and she crawled toward it, and poked her head into the oven.
Then Grettel gave her a shove that sent her right in, shut the iron door, and drew the bolt.
Gracious! How she yelled, it was quite horrible; but Grettel fled, and the wretched old woman was left to roast in her own oven.

covered_sentences: When they had wandered about for some hours they came to a big lake.
"We can't get over," said Hansel; "I see no bridge of any sort or kind."
"Yes, and there's no ferry-boat either," answered Grettel; "but look, there swims a white duck; if I ask her she'll help us over," and she called out:
"Here are two children, who can't cross the lake to get home, because there are no bridge nor ferry boat; Take us upon your white back, And row us over, quack, quack!"
The duck swam toward them, and Hansel got on her back and told his little sister to sit beside him.
"No," answered Grettel, "we should be too heavy a load for the duck: she shall carry us across separately."
